title: Fee Calculation Methodology, Traffic Safety Measures, and SU Tax-Exempt Status
summary: Engineering staff explain the fee calculation: ~30,000 sq ft from curb to curb at unit pricing per the fee schedule, with 14-day renewal periods yielding the $2.6M two-year estimate. The scope could expand if closure extends property-line to property-line. CNS Engineers produced a vehicular/pedestrian traffic plan reviewed by city engineers: detours via Walnut and Marshall streets, fencing, flag personnel at entry/exit points. SU commits to full replacement of sidewalks, curbing, and road repaving upon project completion. A councilor pointedly notes 'Everything but our lost property taxes,' highlighting tension over SU's tax-exempt status and the city's investment in supporting university development.

political_signal.description: Chair requests detailed fee breakdowns per project. 'Everything but our lost property taxes' signals frustration with SU's tax-exempt status and the asymmetry of city costs vs. revenue from university construction.

processing_notes: $2 and $500 are fragments from fee schedule discussion ($2.6M total, $500/sq ft per unit pricing)
